rationale.R1.1: The artifact advances a strong contribution claim ('uniformly improved execution accuracy', 'strictly dominant deployment strategy') that must be interrogated against the data actually reported; the framing of the gap and thesis are in scope for any substantive review.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the 'uniformly improved' claim in the abstract is directly falsified by two reported benchmarks—WikiSQL (89.1% vs. baseline 89.6%) and SParC (64.2% vs. baseline 65.1%)—making logical coherence the single highest-stakes check in this section.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: at least two quantitative claims are misaligned with the evidence the authors themselves present (WikiSQL and SParC show accuracy regressions), and one benchmark (KaggleDBQA) has no baseline comparison at all, undermining the evidentiary basis for the central cost-quality claim.
rationale.R1.6: A generic 'review' instruction centrally includes prose quality; the section's writing is clear but contains argumentative overreach that intersects with language choices ('uniformly', 'strictly dominant'), making polish a meaningful responsibility.
rationale.R1.7: The section relies on externally published baselines (DAIL-SQL/GPT-4, benchmark split provenance) and positions RouteSQL against a competitive landscape; verifying attribution accuracy and benchmark-protocol fidelity is relevant to the review.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review cover technical substance—logical consistency of claims and evidence-claim alignment—or is it scoped to writing and language quality only?
